retrieved_memories.content: When multiple tasks are piling up and interruptions are expected, prioritize by urgency and impact, then use a time-blocking approach to manage interruptions effectively.

When handling interruptions mid-task, pause briefly (5 seconds), assess urgency/safety/deadline, defer noisy or unclear requests, address real issues or set follow-up steps, then return to the original task to maintain focus. Use a 5-second pause to assess the situation before committing to a backup plan, check for safety and practicality, and confirm if the backup will actually get the user to work on time.
To manage work interruptions effectively, pause briefly, ask about safety, deadline, or noise, defer non-urgent noise, handle real issues or set clear next steps, and return to the main task.
Prioritize tasks that are already scheduled or recurring so that they don't require new planning or blocking time; this reduces the risk of cascading delays and keeps the rest of the week on track.

To manage pushy coworkers, use a clear, direct phrasing that redirects urgency: 'If it can wait, put it on the list. If not, tell me what’s actually at risk.' This helps set boundaries and clarifies real risk versus perceived urgency.
